title: ipomoea anceps
intro: Ipomoea anceps, also known as the twin flower or poison vine, is a species of flowering plant in the Convolvulaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ipomoea anceps include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, which is believed to harness its medicinal properties.
